headline: US software stocks slide as IBM, ServiceNow results reignite AI disruption fears
summary: U.S. software stocks fell in premarket trading on Thursday, following quarterly results from IBM and ServiceNow that reignited fears about ‌AI-driven disruption across the sector. International Business Machines said its revenue growth ‌slowed in the first quarter, pressured by weakness in its software business, anchored by its Red ​Hat cloud unit. Growth in the segment slowed to 11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.4% lower.

Growth in the segment slowed to 11.3%, sending the Big Blue's shares 7.4% lower.

headline: ServiceNow Stock Tumbles. The Iran War Is One Reason Why.
summary: ServiceNow  stock was tumbling as investors seemed to want more out of the enterprise software company’s latest results.  ServiceNow posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of 97 cents a share, which was in-line with Wall Street estimates, according to FactSet.  ServiceNow reported first-quarter subscription revenue of $3.67 billion, which was just above estimates of $3.65 billion.

ServiceNow reported first-quarter subscription revenue of $3.67 billion, which was just above estimates of $3.65 billion.

headline: ServiceNow stock sinks on Q1 earnings. CFO discusses AI product growth.
summary: ServiceNow stock (NOW) is sinking by as much as 17% – its largest single-day decrease since January 2020 – on Thursday morning after the workflow automation company reported how the Iran war has impacted its sales growth. ServiceNow president and CFO Gina Mastantuono comes on Market Catalysts to talk more about the software developer's first quarter earnings figures and its AI products.

content.title: ServiceNow stock sinks on Q1 earnings. CFO discusses AI product growth.
content.description:
ServiceNow stock (NOW) is sinking by as much as 17% – its largest single-day decrease since January 2020 – on Thursday morning after the workflow automation company reported how the Iran war has impacted its sales growth.

ServiceNow president and CFO Gina Mastantuono comes on Market Catalysts to talk more about the software developer's first quarter earnings figures and its AI products.
content.summary: ServiceNow stock (NOW) is sinking by as much as 17% – its largest single-day decrease since January 2020 – on Thursday morning after the workflow automation company reported how the Iran war has impacted its sales growth. ServiceNow president and CFO Gina Mastantuono comes on Market Catalysts to talk more about the software developer's first quarter earnings figures and its AI products.

headline: IBM Stock Slides Despite Q1 Earnings Beat As Software's AI Disruption Fears Return
summary: IBM stock fell Thursday after the tech giant's first-quarter earnings beat failed to offset concerns about AI threatening its software business.  The Armonk, N.Y.-based company still expects full-year constant current revenue growth of more than 5%.  IBM Chief Financial Officer James Kavanaugh told analysts on a conference call Wednesday that the company has never raised its annual guidance following Q1, at least not during his nine years in the role.

content.title: IBM Stock Slides Despite Q1 Earnings Beat As Software's AI Disruption Fears Return
content.summary: IBM stock fell Thursday after the tech giant's first-quarter earnings beat failed to offset concerns about AI threatening its software business.  The Armonk, N.Y.-based company still expects full-year constant current revenue growth of more than 5%.  IBM Chief Financial Officer James Kavanaugh told analysts on a conference call Wednesday that the company has never raised its annual guidance following Q1, at least not during his nine years in the role.

content.summary: The Nasdaq slipped from record highs on Thursday after the first big wave of tech earnings failed to save the day.  The Dow dropped 180 points, or 0.4%.  The S&P and Nasdaq finished Wednesday at closing highs.

headline: 2 Large-Cap Stocks with Solid Fundamentals and 1 We Turn Down
